title: Google's Flagship Model Gemini 3.5 Pro Faces Production Delays, Talent Drain and Military Collaboration Trigger Internal Morale Crisis
excerpt: According to news website Axios, six current and former Google DeepMind employees revealed that low employee morale is one of the reasons for the delay in the r
content_html:
According to news website Axios, six current and former Google DeepMind employees revealed that low employee morale is one of the reasons for the delay in the release of Google's flagship model Gemini 3.5 Pro. Bloomberg previously reported that the model has been months behind schedule, and the recent series of small models released by Google have received mixed market reactions. Even Meta AI's head, Wang Tao, sarcastically said on X, "Gemini? I've never heard of it."

Top talent continues to leave, military agreements become a centrifugal force

Google is currently facing serious talent loss. The co-lead of Gemini, Noam Shazeer, has moved to OpenAI, and Nobel Prize-winning chemist John Jumper has joined Anthropic. Several employees resigned due to Google's military cooperation agreement with the U.S. Department of Defense in April, which was frequently mentioned during their exit interviews. Alex Turner, a former research scientist who left due to the military contract, stated that CEO Hassabis appears internally much less frequently than competitors' CEOs, Altman and Amodei. One employee openly said, "We are falling behind," and the Gemini series has failed to rank in the top ten in the OpenRouter large language model rankings.

AI investment leads to negative free cash flow, Google denies morale issues

Google's total spending this year is expected to reach $19 billion, and its free cash flow has turned negative, with growing concerns about the returns on AI capital expenditures. Google denies that employee morale issues have caused the model to lag, stating that the AI talent attrition rate this year is lower than the same period last year, with over 90% of those offered positions accepting them. A Google spokesperson said they are satisfied with the Flash series of models and the future roadmap. However, the report pointed out that while Google can afford to be temporarily overtaken by competitors, it cannot afford talent loss and investor impatience.

content_html_zh:
据新闻网站 Axios 报道，六名谷歌 DeepMind 现任和前任员工透露，员工士气低落是谷歌旗舰机型 Gemini 3.5 Pro 推迟发布的原因之一。 彭博社此前报道称，该机型已经比原计划晚了几个月，而谷歌最近发布的一系列小型机型收到的市场反应不一。 就连Meta AI的负责人王涛也在X上讽刺道：“双子座？ 我从来没有听说过。”

顶尖人才持续离开，军事协议成为离心力

谷歌目前正面临着严重的人才流失。 Gemini 的联合领导者 Noam Shazeer 已转至 OpenAI，诺贝尔奖获得者化学家 John Jumper 已加入 Anthropic。 几名员工因 4 月份谷歌与美国国防部签署的军事合作协议而辞职，这一协议在他们的离职面谈中经常被提及。 因军事合同而离职的前研究科学家亚历克斯·特纳表示，首席执行官哈萨比斯在内部出现的频率远低于竞争对手的首席执行官奥特曼和阿莫迪。 一位员工公开表示，“我们落后了”，Gemini系列在OpenRouter大语言模型排名中未能进入前十。

AI投资导致自由现金流为负 谷歌否认士气问题

谷歌今年的总支出预计将达到 190 亿美元，其自由现金流已转为负值，人们对人工智能资本支出回报的担忧日益加剧。 谷歌否认员工士气问题导致该模型滞后，并表示今年的 AI 人才流失率低于去年同期，超过 90% 的职位接受了他们。 谷歌发言人表示，他们对Flash系列机型以及未来的路线图感到满意。 不过，报告指出，谷歌虽然可以承受暂时被竞争对手超越的后果，但它无法承受人才流失和投资者的不耐烦。
